name: Ethiopia

colors.symbolism: the fertility and richness of the land, hope and labour
peace, justice and harmony among the country's diverse peoples; in older symbolism, religious freedom
strength and the sacrifice of those who died defending Ethiopia's freedom and equality
peace (the disc of the central emblem)

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — green, yellow and red — charged at the centre with a blue disc bearing a yellow five-pointed star outlined and accompanied by five short straight rays (one from each point of the star).
history: The horizontal green-yellow-red tricolour is documented from 1897 under Emperor Menelik II and was the inspiration for the later Pan-African colours adopted across the continent. The current design — with the blue disc and yellow star emblem — was adopted on 6 February 1996 under the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia following the 1991 fall of the Derg regime; the emblem replaced the Lion of Judah (imperial era, ended 1974) and the Marxist–Leninist emblem of the Derg (1974–1991). The tricolour without emblem is also widely used unofficially.

features.symbolism: the green-yellow-red horizontal triband is the original Pan-African colour scheme — Ethiopia, never colonised, became the model for many later African flags
peace and the equal status of all of Ethiopia's nationalities and peoples
the unity and equality of Ethiopia's peoples; the bright future of the country and its diverse nationalities
